Class LoopScopeBlock

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaSourceElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaProgramElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.statement.JavaStatement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.statement.LoopScopeBlock
All Implemented Interfaces:
ExpressionContainer, ModelElement, NonTerminalProgramElement, ProgramElement, SourceElement, Statement, StatementContainer, EqualsModProperty<SourceElement>, ProgramPrefix, SyntaxElement
public class LoopScopeBlock extends JavaStatement implements StatementContainer, ExpressionContainer, ProgramPrefix
Loop scope block. TODO

    • LoopScopeBlock

      public LoopScopeBlock(IProgramVariable iProgramVariable, StatementBlock body)
      creates a loop-scope block
      Parameters:
      iProgramVariable - the IProgramVariable to indicate whether the scope should be left or continuued
      body - the StatementBlock inside the loop scope (the actual loop body)

    • LoopScopeBlock

      public LoopScopeBlock(ExtList children)
      Synchronized block.
      Parameters:
      children - a list with all children

      the statement with the given index.

    • getFirstElement

      public SourceElement getFirstElement()
      Description copied from class: JavaSourceElement
      Finds the source element that occurs first in the source. The default implementation returns this element, which is correct for all terminal program elements, and many non terminals such as statements and prefixed operators.
      Specified by:
      getFirstElement in interface SourceElement
      Overrides:
      getFirstElement in class JavaSourceElement
      Returns:
      the first source element in the syntactical representation of this element, may be equals to this element.
      See Also:
      • JavaSourceElement.toSource()
      • JavaSourceElement.getStartPosition()

      v - the Visitor
